name: Vulnerability Exposure Level
natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities are on internet-exposed or bastion assets?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily table which contains one row per vulnerability instance per asset per day with exposure flags
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL and exposure_flag is internet_exposed or bastion
Group by snapshot_date and exposure_flag to compute daily counts of critical vulnerabilities for each exposure type
Optionally compute an exposure-weighted risk score per day by multiplying count by mean CVE risk score and sum by snapshot_date
Produce a 30-day trend series and annotate increases or decreases compared to the prior 30-day window
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Requires silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily to exist; gold table not yet available

name: Vulnerability Acceptance Tracking
natural_language_question: How many vulnerabilities are currently risk-accepted, and which acceptances expire in the next 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_qualys_vuln_acceptances_daily table containing normalized acceptance records per day
Filter to acceptances where acceptance_end is within the next 30 days to flag expiring acceptances
Group by snapshot_date and acceptance_status to compute daily counts of active and expiring acceptances
Also group by severity to surface how many accepted vulnerabilities are CRITICAL
Create a 30-day trend showing accepted counts and upcoming expirations
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Requires joining vuln_accept_schema and vuln_tracker_schema to build the silver table

name: CVE Exploitability Metrics
natural_language_question: How many of our open vulnerabilities are known to be exploited in the wild, and what is their EPSS distribution?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_snyk_cves_daily table which enriches CVEs with EPSS and exploited-in-wild flags
Join silver_snyk_cves_daily to silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily (or vulnerability_instances_schema) to count open instances linked to exploited CVEs
Group by snapshot_date and exploited_in_wild flag to produce daily counts and EPSS distribution buckets
Compute percent of open vulnerabilities tied to CVEs with EPSS > 0.5 and trend that percent over 30 days
Produce EPSS percentile distribution time series for monitoring changes
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Requires daily CVE enrichment (silver_snyk_cves_daily) and join to vulnerability instances

name: CVE Risk Score Trend
natural_language_question: Which CVEs have the highest risk scores this week, and how has their rank changed compared to last month?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_snyk_cve_risks_daily table containing daily risk_score and rank per CVE
Filter to the week of interest and select top 10 CVEs by risk_score for the current week
Compare each CVE's current rank to its rank 30 days prior to compute rank_change and add that delta to the row
Aggregate mean risk score by asset_group per snapshot_date to show asset-group-level risk trends
Produce a weekly time series chart of top CVE risk scores and their month-over-month rank movement
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
kpis_covered: Mean risk score by asset group
implementation_note: Requires historical daily risk snapshots to compute rank deltas

name: Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality
natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities exist on our most critical assets, and how has that changed?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_by_asset_daily which includes asset_criticality and severity per daily snapshot
Filter to assets with criticality marked as HIGH or CRITICAL and to vulnerabilities with severity CRITICAL
Group by snapshot_date and asset_criticality to compute daily counts of critical vulnerabilities on critical assets
Compute weighted vuln counts by applying asset criticality weights and produce a 30- and 90-day trend
Highlight assets that contributed the largest increases in critical vuln counts over the selected period
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Requires features.json to provide asset criticality weights; silver table must be built first

name: CVE Mitigation Influence
natural_language_question: Which CVEs have the highest mitigation efficacy, and how does that affect our prioritization?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_snyk_cve_mitigation_daily which contains mitigation_score per CVE and linked instance counts
For each CVE, calculate the mitigated risk delta by multiplying mitigation efficacy by the number of affected instances and their asset criticality weight
Rank CVEs daily by mitigated risk delta to produce a prioritized list
Group by snapshot_date and produce time series for top mitigatable CVEs and mean mitigation efficacy over time
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Requires linking CVE mitigation signals to vulnerability instances and asset weights in silver tables

name: Vulnerability Count by Severity
natural_language_question: How many critical and high severity vulnerabilities do we have, and how has that changed over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which provides a daily snapshot of active vulnerability instances
Filter to severity tiers CRITICAL and HIGH and to the last 30 days of snapshot_date
Group by severity and snapshot_date to compute daily counts for each severity tier
Compute a 30-day rolling sum or average and compare current 30-day total to the prior 30-day period to show change
Produce mean time to remediate by severity by joining with vuln_tracker_schema data on remediation timestamps
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
kpis_covered: Mean time to remediate by severity
implementation_note: Requires vuln_tracker_schema for remediation timestamps to compute MTTR

name: Vulnerability Fix Backlog by Team
natural_language_question: Which teams have the largest backlog of unresolved vulnerabilities and how has each team's backlog trended over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_qualys_team_backlog_daily table which links vulnerability instances to assigned teams and fix records
Filter to open vulnerabilities and compute open_vulnerability_count and overdue_fix_count per team for each snapshot_date
Compute avg_days_open on a per-team basis using remediation timestamps from cvedata/fix_instances_schema_pkey
Produce a 90-day time series per team and flag teams whose backlog or overdue counts are increasing month-over-month
implementation_note: Requires cvedata/vuln_tracker_schema and fix instances data to compute overdue and days open metrics

name: CVE patch adoption latency
natural_language_question: For CVEs that have patches available, how long does it take on average for teams to apply them and which assets lag?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_patch_adoption_daily which contains per-instance adoption_latency computed from patch release to application
Filter to CVEs with patch_available true and to the asset groups of interest
Group by snapshot_date and asset_group to compute daily median and mean adoption latency and percent adopted within 14 days
Identify assets that are persistent laggards by flagging those above a configured latency percentile across a rolling window
Produce a 30- and 90-day trend series to show improvement or regression in patch adoption latency
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Patch release dates must be available in the vulnerability instance or external patch feed to compute latency

name: CVE patch availability rate
natural_language_question: What portion of our vulnerability instances have an available patch or documented fix version, and which CVEs remain without fixes?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_patch_availability_daily which flags vulnerability instances with fix_version or patch_record
For each snapshot_date compute pct_instances_with_fix_versions and pct_instances_with_patch_records by dividing instances with flags by total instances
Group results by snapshot_date and CVE to produce time series of patch availability and lists of patchless CVEs
Break down patchless instance counts by asset_group and severity to prioritize remediation efforts
implementation_note: Requires reliable patch metadata in vulnerability instance records; silver table must be populated daily
